operation: First, an XZ orientation workplane is created and tagged as 'base'. A rectangular box is generated on this workplane with dimensions 100 (X-axis) × 95 (Y-axis) × 13 (Z-axis), with centering disabled on the Z-axis so the bottom face of the box rests on the base workplane. A new workplane offset 13mm from the tagged base workplane (aligned to the top face of the box) is created and tagged as 'holeplane'. An 80×34 construction rectangle is drawn on this workplane, its four vertices are selected, and 8mm diameter through holes are cut at each selected vertex through the full thickness of the box.
geometry: This model is a uniform-thickness rectangular prism plate. Four identical circular through holes are symmetrically arranged at the four corners of a smaller invisible rectangle centered on the large flat face of the plate, running completely through the plate's thickness. All edges are sharp right angles with no secondary features like fillets or chamfers.
function: This is a standard structural mounting plate designed for mechanical fastening. The four corner through holes allow it to be securely bolted to other components or surfaces, serving as a base bracket, adapter interface, or support plate for equipment and assembly applications.
